attacks.summary: !/usr/bin/python from future import print function import socket,ssl from BaseHTTPServer import BaseHTTPRequestHandler,HTTPServer from websocket import create connection, WebSocket from urlparse import parse qs import argparse import os LOOP BACK PORT NUMBER = 8000 def FuzzWebSocket(fuzz value): print(fuzz value) ws.send(ws message.replace("[FUZZ]", str(fuzz value[0]))) result = ws.recv() return result def LoadMessage(file): file contents = "" try: if os.path.isfile(file): f = open(file,'r') file contents = f.read() f.close() except: print("Error reading file: %s" % file) exit() return file contents class myWebServer(BaseHTTPRequestHandler): Handler for the GET requests def do GET(self): qs = parse qs(self.path[2:]) fuzz value = qs['fuzz'] result = FuzzWebSocket(fuzz value) self.send response(200) self.send header('Content type','text/html') self.end headers() self.wfile.write(result) return parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Web Socket Harness: Use traditional tools to asse
